title: edmundmiller/fastq-screen pipeline parameters
description: A tool for multi-genome mapping and quality control

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to FASTQ files (can use glob patterns like '*.fastq.gz')
input.help_text:
Use this to specify the location of your input FASTQ files. For example:

```bash
--input '*.fastq.gz'
```

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

fastq_screen_options.title: FastQ Screen options
fastq_screen_options.description: Options specific to FastQ Screen analysis

conf.description: Path to FastQ Screen configuration file
conf.help_text: Configuration file containing database paths and aligner settings

subset.description: Process only a subset of reads for faster analysis
subset.help_text: Specify the number of reads to process from each FASTQ file

aligner.description: Alignment tool to use for mapping
aligner.help_text: Choose the alignment tool for mapping reads to reference genomes

threads.description: Number of threads to use for alignment
threads.help_text: Number of CPU threads to use for the alignment step

bisulfite.description: Enable bisulfite mode for methylation analysis

tag.description: Add genome tags to read headers
tag.help_text: Tag reads with their mapping genome in the output

nohits.description: Output reads that didn't map to any genome
nohits.help_text: Include unmapped reads in the output

illumina1_3.help_text: Enable for older Illumina quality score format

filter.description: Filter reads based on mapping results
filter.help_text: Filter string using digits 0-5 and hyphens to specify which reads to output

pass.help_text: Only output reads with mapping quality above this threshold

inverse.help_text: Output reads that don't match the filter criteria

top.description: Process only the top portion of reads
top.help_text: Process only the first N reads or percentage of reads

get_genomes.help_text: Automatically download and configure standard genome databases

add_genome.description: Add a custom genome to the analysis
add_genome.help_text: Path to custom genome for inclusion in the screening

aligner_options.title: Aligner-specific options
aligner_options.description: Options to pass to specific alignment tools

bowtie_opts.help_text: Extra command line options to pass to Bowtie

bowtie2_opts.help_text: Extra command line options to pass to Bowtie2

bismark_opts.help_text: Extra command line options to pass to Bismark

bwa_opts.help_text: Extra command line options to pass to BWA

minimap2_opts.help_text: Extra command line options to pass to minimap2

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

validationFailUnrecognisedParams.description: Validation of parameters fails when an unrecognised parameter is found.

validationLenientMode.description: Validation of parameters in lenient more.

validationSchemaIgnoreParams.description: A comma separated string of inputs the schema validation should ignore

max_job_request_options.title: Max job request options
max_job_request_options.description: Set the top limit for requested resources for any single job.
max_job_request_options.help_text:
If you are running on a smaller system, a pipeline step requesting more resources than are available may cause the Nextflow to stop the run with an error. These options allow you to cap the maximum resources requested by any single job so that the pipeline will run on your system.

Note that you can not _increase_ the resources requested by any job using these options. For that you will need your own configuration file. See [the nf-core website](https://nf-co.re/usage/configuration) for details.

max_cpus.description: Maximum number of CPUs that can be requested for any single job.
max_cpus.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the CPU requirement for each process. Should be an integer e.g. `--max_cpus 1`

max_memory.description: Maximum amount of memory that can be requested for any single job.
max_memory.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the memory requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. `--max_memory '8.GB'`

max_time.description: Maximum amount of time that can be requested for any single job.
max_time.help_text: Use to set an upper-limit for the time requirement for each process. Should be a string in the format integer-unit e.g. `--max_time '2.h'`
